siapsez.description: Modern web platform that is digitizing and automating the entire administrative ecosystem of the state’s public education system, from teacher management to payroll and fleet operations.

data.description: Led the complete frontend turnaround of SIAPSEZ, a mission-critical internal platform for the Zacatecas Government Department of Education. Rescued a half-finished project and delivered a full redesign and rebuild using TypeScript, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS, establishing a scalable design system from the ground up.

Architected and implemented the core user-facing modules, including secure authentication, dynamic navigation, payment calculation workflows, fleet administration, system configuration, and multiple internal dashboards—ensuring a consistent, professional, and highly usable experience across the entire application.

Led a team of frontend developers, setting coding standards, mentoring on large-scale React/Next.js best practices, and driving architectural decisions that significantly improved performance, maintainability, and developer velocity in a complex enterprise environment.
Currently in production with active development and iterative releases. The platform is already being used by government personnel, with the official statewide rollout scheduled for August 2026.

hadassa.description: Full-stack web application built solo to streamline appointment scheduling, staff management, and daily operations for a local beauty lash salon, delivering a modern, user-friendly tool that replaced manual processes.

data.description: Designed and developed Hadassa end-to-end as a solo full-stack engineer — a production-ready web app for a lash/beauty salon using TypeScript, Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS, Express-style API routes in Next.js, MySQL, and Vercel for seamless CI/CD and hosting.

Architected core business features including an interactive calendar for booking & managing appointments, real-time availability viewer (public-facing), appointment actions dashboard (confirm/cancel/reschedule), admin panels for personnel management, services catalog, and secure authentication system — all with clean, responsive UI/UX tailored to salon staff.

Handled complete technical ownership from initial UI/UX design and wireframing, through backend architecture & database schema design (MySQL), to deployment and post-launch maintenance; delivered the entire MVP in 6 months while incorporating client feedback for iterative improvements.

Live in production since completion, actively used daily by the salon team for scheduling and operations, with ongoing occasional updates and enhancements based on real-world usage — demonstrating long-term reliability and maintainability.
